name: Alpine.js (Lightweight JS Interactivity)

performance.notes: ~15KB gzipped; no virtual DOM; direct DOM manipulation; extremely fast

agent_readiness.notes: Lightweight JavaScript framework for sprinkling interactivity into HTML templates. Declarative directives: x-data, x-bind, x-on, x-show, x-if, x-for, x-model, x-text, x-transition, x-cloak. ~15KB gzipped. No build step required. Perfect for HTMX companion. Server-rendered HTML + Alpine.js for interactivity without full SPA framework. Laravel Livewire companion. Tailwind CSS complement. 'Tailwind for JS' — utility-first interactivity.

gotchas: Not suited for complex SPAs (use React/Vue/Svelte instead)
State management for large apps becomes complex
best_when: Server-rendered HTML apps needing JavaScript interactivity
Replacing jQuery or vanilla JS for simple interactions
avoid_when: Complex SPAs with heavy client-side routing
summary: Alpine.js adds JavaScript interactivity to HTML with declarative directives (x-data, x-bind, x-on, x-show, x-for, x-model). ~15KB gzipped, no build step. The 'Tailwind for JavaScript' — utility-first frontend without SPA complexity. Perfect companion to HTMX or server-rendered HTML in Laravel/Rails/Django. Replaces jQuery for simple interactions. Not suited for complex SPAs.
